title: pyosys API: Silent aborts (segfault/assert())
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

A number of Python API calls can 'crash' the Python interpreter due to segfaults or assert() exits from the yosys kernel side.

I've collected them in a binder with a fairly recent pyosys build:

[![Binder](https://mybinder.org/badge_logo.svg)](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/hackfin/myhdl.git/jupyosys?filepath=src%2Fmyhdl%2Fmyhdl-yosys%2Fexample%2Fipynb%2Fpyosys_issues.ipynb)

## Expected behavior

Return a Python exception.

## Actual behavior

Python interpreter or IPython kernel terminate without notice. When called from the shell, the error can be guessed. Inside IPython, the kernel dies non-verbosely and it seems impossible to debug the problem through stderr capture.


## Comment

Maybe it would be time to start a test suite for the pyosys API. Any ideas, opinions on `py.test`? Testing might get tricky due the module crashing (i.e. aborting the test)
